TextENGNew: Naturally, once Sunday ends, Monday comes.
And with it... the long-awaited start of my life as class rep~!
...That's what I have to keep telling myself, or the urge to run away would be too pathetic to bear. I really need to steel my resolve already.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: And sure enough, I was immediately tasked with my first duty as class rep...
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "...My historic first assignment is hauling bags of lime~~?"
changeReason: Old says 'carrying chalk'; new says 'hauling bags of lime'--chalk vs lime are different substances (chalk=CaCO3, lime=CaO/Ca(OH)2).

TextENGNew: "Come on, quit complaining and carry!@k@vS14/03/210300364. There's still plenty more."
Mion said that while hoisting a huge bag of lime with a grunt. I, being told 'You're a man, aren't you?!' -- which was true but felt somehow unreasonable -- was forced to carry two at once.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: But lime powder is only used for drawing lines on the field... so why on earth do we need this many?
changeReason: Old says 'chalk used for drawing lines'; new says 'lime powder is only used for drawing lines'--different substance named (chalk vs lime).

TextENGNew: "Looks like Chie-sensei messed up the order.@k@vS14/03/210300366. Was supposed to be four bags, but it went up a digit to forty."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: W-wait... That's one hell of a misorder, isn't it?!
"Aahahaha!@k@vS14/03/210300368. It's fine! They're consumables anyway!@k@vS14/03/210300369. The principal said he'd let it slide."
What a magnanimous principal...
"But Chie-sensei couldn't forgive herself for it,@k@vS14/03/210300371. so she imposed a penalty on herself."
"A penalty...?@k@vS14/01/210100507. What kind?"
"No curry for a week."
I see... So that's why Chie-sensei had that deflated, devastated look earlier. A punishment on par with 'stop breathing.' Still, you had to admire her sense of responsibility.
...I just hope she doesn't go through withdrawal during class and take it out on us.
changeReason: Old says 'she was going through withdrawal symptoms in class and taking it out on us' (already happened); new says 'I just hope she doesn't go through withdrawal during class' (hypothetical)--past vs future.

TextENGNew: "By the way, Mion...@k@vS14/01/210100509. Have you really been doing work like this by yourself all along?"
"Hm?@k@vS14/03/210300374. Ah, well... sometimes, yeah.@k@vS14/03/210300375. But usually Rena and the others pitch in."
Mion said that with an easygoing laugh.
...But now that I thought about it, whenever PE equipment ran low, it would somehow be fully restocked within a few days. I'd always noticed that.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I'd assumed the supplier or a teacher handled it... but it had been Mion all along.
I was reminded once again of how much thankless behind-the-scenes work Mion had shouldered as class rep.
"Thanks, Mion."
"Huh?@k@vS14/03/210300377. What, what, for what?"
"Just, you know... for handling tough jobs like this all on your own."
"Ah... ahahaha, it's nothing! This is totally easy, okay?"
"This ol' man may not look it, but I'm surprisingly athletic~!"
"No, there's nothing surprising about that.@k@vS14/01/210100513. It's exactly what it looks like."
In fact, if she ever called herself the brainy type, I'd point my finger and yell 'DOUBT!' at the top of my lungs.
"...Hmph!@k@vS14/03/210300381. In that case, Kei-chan, why don't you handle the rest solo?@k@vS14/03/210300382. It'll be great exercise!"
"Oh, no, I was kidding...@k@vS14/01/210100515. Whoa?!"
Without warning, Mion hurled her lime bag right onto my stack.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Th-three bags is already back-breaking, and then adding sudden extra weight... m-my spine...!
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Well well, what's the matter, Class Rep Maebara?@k@vS14/03/210300384. Folding already~?"
"You... demon...!"
"Well, duh -- I'm a child of the Sonozaki family, after all~?@k@vS14/03/210300386. Aahahahaha~!"
changeReason: Old says 'Of course I'm a demon, I'm a child of the Sonozaki family'; new says 'Well, duh -- I'm a child of the Sonozaki family' omitting the 'demon' self-reference.

TextENGNew: Mion laughed cheerfully. ...Was it my imagination, or was she a bit more fired up than usual? What was that about?
"You know... wouldn't this go faster if we called in Rena and the others...?"
"What's that~?@k@vS14/03/210300388. Saying this ol' man isn't enough help for you?"
...You're not even helping right now.
I groused internally, but having someone beside me was still better than trudging along in silence. ...Even if the load was heavier for it.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Well, I did think about asking at first...@k@vS14/03/210300390. But when I brought it up, she said 'I don't want to get in Mii-chan's waaay~ hau~' and ducked out."
changeReason: Old quotes Rena saying only that she doesn't want to get in Mion's way; new adds 'and ducked out'--additional action described.

TextENGNew: "What's that supposed to mean?@k@vS14/01/210100519. I don't get it."
"...Seriously?"
Mion shot me a flat, exasperated stare. Even so, I genuinely didn't understand...
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "Wh-what?"
"You can handle one more, right?@k@vS14/03/210300395. I'll go grab it!"
"Ngaah?!"
W-wait! Four bags is absolutely impossible -- completely out of the question!!
But Mion had already sprinted to the mountain of lime bags at the school gate with blinding speed. She plucked one up and came charging back, then launched it at me as if it were nothing --
"Gwah?!"
"Oh~?@k@vS14/03/210300397. Good catch, Kei-chan!@k@vS14/03/210300398. I figured you'd dodge!"
D-don't be ridiculous! If I hadn't caught a lime bag thrown at that speed, it would've exploded on the ground and turned me completely white! Are you trying to do a slapstick routine?!
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Well, now that your punishment game is done, Kei-chan...@k@vS14/03/210300400. let's get these stored away~!"

Leaving me struggling to keep the teetering pile of lime bags from collapsing, Mion turned on her heel and marched toward the storehouse. ...Was it my imagination, or were her footsteps unusually heavy and stompy?
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I don't understand... She seemed to be in a good mood a moment ago. So why the punishment game?
"F-finally~~..."
Completely drenched in sweat, Mion and I delivered the last bag of lime.
In the end, I only had to make the first trip alone. After that, Mion carried one bag per round trip.
...For the record, I was carrying three. My legs were shot, and I was practically falling apart. ...Next period was definitely going to be naptime.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "Heave... ho..."
I carefully set down the three lime bags in front of the storehouse and sank to the ground. The sweat that had been beading all over my body immediately burst free like a waterfall.
"Done~.@k@vS14/01/210100526. Thanks for the help, Mion."
"Whoa, hold that thought.@k@vS14/03/210300402. You can only say 'done' after we've stacked these inside."
With that, Mion wrenched the heavy door open with a rattling groan, grabbed one of the bags, and hauled it inside.
Seeing that, I got to my feet, brushed the dirt off my shorts, and lifted a lime bag.
The room had been sealed shut, and the air inside was thick with stale, muggy warmth and a musty smell that hit my nose and made me grimace.
Peering into the dim interior, I could see Mion looking around for a good spot to stack the bags.
"-- Ah, watch out there, Mion!"

My warning came too late -- Mion tripped over a track hurdle lying on the floor and went down. The impact knocked the lime bag from her arms and it burst open, sending a cloud of white powder billowing into the air.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "*cough* *cough*...@k@vS14/03/210300406. Ow~, that hurt..."
"You okay, Mion?!"
Through the white haze I could barely make out Mion coughing. I rushed to check on her, groping for the storehouse light switch --
"?! Don't, Kei-chan!@k@vS14/03/210300408. Don't turn on the lights!"
The moment I turned toward her, a round object hit me square in the face and I toppled backward with a yelp -- fortunately landing on a stack of lime bags.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to mion, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "Wh-what was that for, Mion?!"
"S-sorry... Did that hurt?"
Mion came stumbling out, coughing and covered in white powder. From her apologetic face I could tell she hadn't meant any harm, but getting nailed by a volleyball out of nowhere was still a shock.
"Kei-chan, you absolutely cannot flip a light switch in an enclosed space when fine powder is suspended in the air.@k@vS14/03/210300411. It's called a dust explosion -- haven't you heard of it?"
Wait... really? Then if I'd actually turned on the light just now...
"The instant you flipped the switch, the spark would ignite the powder.@k@vS14/03/210300413. The roof of the storehouse would blow off.@k@vS14/03/210300414. And you can guess what happens to us."
changeReason: Old says 'sparks would fly...The roof of this storehouse would be blown away. And we'd be right here watching the fireworks'; new says 'the spark would ignite the powder. The roof would blow off. And you can guess what happens to us'--different phrasing of the consequence (fireworks=humorous vs 'you can guess'=ominous).

TextENGNew: "Y-you can't be serious!@k@vS14/01/210100533. If we took a direct hit from something like that, we'd be done for!"
"That's why I stopped you."
"...Ah, but throwing the ball was overkill.@k@vS14/03/210300417. Sorry about that."
Still... depending on how you looked at it, Mion had just saved my life. I should be thanking her, not the other way around.
"Oh... but Mion, how did you know about something like that?@k@vS14/01/210100535. They don't teach that in school, do they?"

"Nah, I learned it a long time ago.@k@vS14/03/210300419. During the anti-dam protests, a military instructor taught us all sorts of things.@k@vS14/03/210300420. Dust explosions, first aid, that kind of stuff."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Military...?"
"Aahahaha!@k@vS14/03/210300422. When I say military, it's not as extreme as it sounds."
"Most of it was just extensions of what firefighters know -- emergency first aid, that sort of thing.@k@vS14/03/210300424. Less about fighting and more about staying alive when things go south."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Mion laughed it off.
Indeed, in extraordinary circumstances like natural disasters or armed conflict, military knowledge was more valuable as a means of saving allies than defeating enemies.
And for Mion and her people, who had faced off against a massive organization during the dam protests, those skills must have been absolutely essential.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: But for someone like me, who had lived his whole life far removed from such things, the word 'military' still carried a heavy weight.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: In that sense, I couldn't help but feel the gulf between myself and Mion, who had survived Hinamizawa's past crisis.
"Hm?@k@vS14/03/210300426. What's wrong, Kei-chan?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/01/210100538. Oh, nothing. I was just genuinely impressed, that's all."
"Hehehe, don't underestimate this old man's skills."
"In fact, I'm the one who taught Satoko about traps -- including dust explosions!@k@vS14/03/210300429. My grades may not be great, but when it comes to practical skills, I'm top of the class!"
"...Are you not embarrassed to admit that?"
Oh, she turned red.
"Aah~, forget it!@k@vS14/03/210300431. My hair is all caked with lime now~!@k@vS14/03/210300432. I'm going to go wash it off! Be back soon!"
changeReason: Old says 'I'm covered in chalk'; new says 'My hair is all caked with lime.' Old adds 'Mind if I wash up a bit?'; new says 'I'm going to go wash it off! Be back soon!' (statement vs question).

TextENGNew: "Sure.@k@vS14/01/210100541. ...Actually, you can just head back, Mion.@k@vS14/01/210100542. I'll handle the rest of the stacking."
"Really?"
"...Oh, then I'll bring you something to drink."
"Nice, something ice cold would be perfect."
"Got it -- one ice-cold mayonnaise coming up~."
"I'm not a mayo freak!!"
"Aahahahaha~!"
Mion ran off toward the school building, laughing all the way. Watching her go, my mind drifted back to what she'd told me earlier.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...Hinamizawa... and the Sonozakis... huh."
What Akane-san had told me at last evening's banquet flickered through my mind.
changeReason: Old says 'I thought back on what I heard at the banquet yesterday evening'; new says 'What Akane-san had told me at last evening's banquet'--new names the speaker (Akane-san) while old does not.

TextENGNew: In the Sonozaki family, it was tradition for the designated heir to be given a name containing the character for 'demon.'
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Like the current family head, Grandmother Oryou.
And Akane-san, who had apparently lost her claim to succession. Her original name had been something that included that character too.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: And then -- Mion.
Those names were proof of standing as the de facto leaders of the Three Families who governed Hinamizawa, once known as 'Onigafuchi' -- and a testament to the weight of that responsibility.
Mion was no exception. I'd heard she had received rigorous training as the next family head from her grandmother Oryou.
And during the dam protests, Mion had stood at the front as the practical leader, fighting alongside the villagers.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: I found myself wondering. While Mion had been fighting alongside the adults for the sake of the village... what had I been doing with my life?
That was five years before I came here.
I had been nothing but selfish, stupid, and completely indifferent to the people around me...
Such a hopeless kid that I was ashamed to even look back.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: The gap between myself and Mion, who had endured such a bitter struggle, felt as vast as the distance between heaven and earth. I couldn't close it easily.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: But that was exactly why I wanted to try harder... to do something meaningful.
If I could manage that, then surely...
changeReason: New translation adds substantial content not in the original.

TextENGNew: "...Haah."
With a single exhale, I grabbed the lime bag beside me.
First things first -- finish this job. Before I knew it, my arms and legs, which had felt so leaden, seemed just a little bit lighter.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Before long, classes ended for the day.
Today's club activities were postponed because Mion had a part-time shift.
"Yeah, Uncle Yoshirou's place is short-staffed.@k@vS14/03/210300438. So I got called in as emergency backup."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Uncle Yoshirou... That meant Angel Mort.
Come to think of it, she'd stopped hiding her waitressing job lately.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: She used to get flustered about it: 'Don't you dare tell Rena and the others! If you do, I'll put you through all forty-eight killing techniques~!'
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Incidentally, the very next day after she'd sworn me to secrecy, Shion spilled the beans and it became common knowledge among the club members.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: Until the real culprit was identified, Mion assumed I was the leak, and as 'thanks,' I was subjected to twenty-six of those forty-eight techniques.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: ...Let's not think about that.
Anyway, Rena had already headed home early saying she was going treasure hunting. Well, going home alone once in a while wasn't so bad.
"-- Keiichi."
"Hm?"
I turned at the voice behind me, and there was Rika-chan.
"Are you heading home now?"
"Yeah, I am."
"Then let's walk together."
Together...? If I remembered right, Rika-chan's house was in the opposite direction from mine.
"...I have something to discuss.@k@vS14/05/210500383. Will you walk with me?"
That's right. The night before last, Rika-chan had made that mysterious request, and I'd promised to help.
I hadn't forgotten, but since then Rika-chan had been acting completely normal, so I'd never found the right moment to bring it up.
"Got it.@k@vS14/01/210100552. Where to?"
"It might be a bit out of the way, but...@k@vS14/05/210500385. would Furude Shrine be all right?"
And so we climbed the stone steps and arrived at the Furude Shrine grounds.
The shrine building itself was in full sunlight, so Rika-chan found a cool spot in the shade and perched on a large stone. I sat on a flat rock beside her.
I pulled out the bottles of cider and grape juice we'd bought at a candy shop along the way, and popped them open with the bottle opener from the assembly hall. I handed the grape juice to Rika-chan.
changeReason: Old says 'popped them open with a corkscrew from the assembly hall'; new says 'popped them open with the bottle opener from the assembly hall'--corkscrew vs bottle opener are different tools.

TextENGNew: "Hm?@k@vS14/01/210100554. What's wrong, Rika-chan?"
"...Mii, it's nothing.@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1119. Keiichi, would you mind swapping? I'd rather have the cider."
"Huh? Sure, here.@k@vS14/01/VT4Ba_keiichi1008. Here you go."
I swapped my cider for her grape juice. ...The sweet, rich flavor was wonderfully refreshing after sweating in the heat.
From the tall trees lining the grounds came the deafening chorus of cicadas. A picture-perfect, idyllic country scene.
However.
Rika-chan, usually so cheerful, had been fidgeting with the gravel at her feet, her face tense and downturned since our walk here. Seeing that, I pulled myself together.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "So, Rika-chan.@k@vS14/01/210100556. ...What did you want to talk about?"
"...Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500390. What is the worst possible future you can imagine?"
Rika-chan looked up at me as she asked.
"Huh? The worst...?"
The sudden question caught me off guard.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: Before, Rika-chan had asked me about the 'things I hold dear.' And now she was asking about the polar opposite -- the 'worst possible future'...?
I thought for a moment about what would be 'the worst' for me. Then I answered.
"Let's see...@k@vS14/01/210100559. I think I answered you about 'precious things' before, right?@k@vS14/01/210100560. The worst would be losing all of them."
That's right. Mion, Rena, Satoko, Shion... and Rika-chan.
If those irreplaceable friends suddenly vanished, I'd be thrown back into that hollow, purposeless existence I'd had in Tokyo...
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Just imagining it filled my chest with a sick, heavy dread.
"Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500392. Do you like Satoko?"
"Huh?"
Another strange question, one right after the other. ...But Rika-chan's expression remained dead serious. She clearly wanted a sincere answer.
"...Yeah, I love her."
So I answered directly, without hiding or hemming.
She was a little cheeky, loved mischief, put on a mature act but was secretly a big baby...
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: I couldn't even count how many times her awful traps had caught me. And hearing that smug, triumphant laugh of hers had made me think 'why you little...' more than once.
But.
Satoko was genuinely kind and caring. And the strength she showed -- never letting a hint of darkness show despite having no parents -- was something I truly admired.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Having her around was fun. When she wasn't there, it was lonely.
We hadn't known each other long, but... she had already become someone I couldn't imagine my life without.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "If that same Satoko...@k@vS14/05/VT4Ba_rika1121. were going to be badly hurt in the near future... what would you do?"
"?!@k@vS14/01/210100564. Isn't it obvious?! I'd hunt down whoever did it and deck them!!"
The answer flew out before I could think. I worried it sounded too violent, but when I looked at Rika-chan, she simply smiled her 'Nipah~' and nodded.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "I'm glad the Keiichi of this world feels that way too."
"...So please, lend me your strength to save Satoko."
"Huh?@k@vS14/01/210100566. Y-yeah... of course.@k@vS14/01/210100567. But what exactly is going to happen to Satoko...?"
"Have you heard anything about Satoko's uncle?"
I shook my head silently. I'd known she had no parents, but beyond that, something had always stopped me from asking about Satoko's family.
"Satoko has an uncle named Teppei.@k@vS14/05/210500399. He currently lives in Okinomiya with a woman named Rina."
Rika-chan explained in a calm, measured tone. ...The slight grimace on her face told me she didn't have much fondness for this Teppei character.
"On paper, Teppei currently has custody of Satoko.@k@vS14/05/210500401. ...But when they lived together until last year, he and his wife treated Satoko and her brother terribly."
"Treated badly...? How so?"
When I asked, Rika-chan continued with a bitter expression. What she described was enough to leave me speechless, my fists trembling with rage.
After their parents died, Satoko and her brother Satoshi were taken in by their aunt and uncle.
...Though since Satoko's family apparently left behind some assets, 'moved in' might be more accurate than 'taken in.'
This aunt and uncle had been notorious troublemakers in the village. And they had treated Satoko and Satoshi as punching bags for their frustrations -- abusing them in every way imaginable.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to satoshi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: Satoshi, the older brother, had shielded the young and helpless Satoko from the worst of it, enduring the abuse himself.
Then, on the night of last year's Watanagashi, the cruel aunt was murdered. Satoshi, suspected of the killing, vanished as if driven out -- and was never seen again.
To think that bright, cheerful Satoko had a past like that...!
I ground my teeth.
Anger, hatred... and something close to murderous fury. A pitch-black tide of emotion surged up from inside me.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: He had hurt Satoko. That alone was unforgivable -- a crime worthy of death a thousand times over. And that scum had abandoned Satoko to go live it up with some woman...
"-- Keiichi."
Rika-chan gently placed her hand on my tightly clenched fist.
Her touch was soft and warm. When I turned to look, Rika-chan silently shook her head.
"I'm glad you're this angry on Satoko's behalf.@k@vS14/05/210500404. Truly glad."
"...But what I'm going to ask of you isn't revenge or violence.@k@vS14/05/210500406. It's something much harder -- and something that will lead to Satoko's greatest happiness."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to keiichi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "Greatest... happiness?"
"Keiichi.@k@vS14/05/210500408. ...Even if Teppei disappears, a world where Keiichi is gone won't make Satoko happy.@k@vS14/05/210500409. That's how important you are."
changeReason: Old says 'Even if Teppei disappears, Satoko can never be happy in a world without you. So we have to find a way that Satoko can be happy while you're still here'; new says 'Even if Teppei disappears, a world where Keiichi is gone won't make Satoko happy. That's how important you are.' -- old explicitly mentions finding a way, new emphasizes Keiichi's importance.

TextENGNew: Listening to Rika-chan's words,
something nameless stirred deep in my chest.
A world... without me.
In that instant, the ugly conclusion that came from rashly trying to eliminate Teppei forced itself into my mind.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: ...My boiling emotions slowly cooled. Hypothetical as it was, I could clearly feel that this path was not the right one.
"Understood. ...What then?"
"Teppei is currently living in Okinomiya,@k@vS14/05/210500411. ...but he'll likely come back to Hinamizawa soon.@k@vS14/05/210500412. Because his partner Rina... will be murdered."
"Mur... dered...?!"
The word 'murder' made me recoil.
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "Prophecy or not... 'murdered' isn't something you can say lightly.@k@vS14/01/210100574. And what does this Rina person being killed have to do with Satoko?"
changeReason: New translation adds reference to satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "Rina currently works at a bar in the Sonozaki business network.@k@vS14/05/210500414. And within a few days, she and some accomplices will attempt to rob the protection money collected from those affiliated establishments."
changeReason: Old says 'she's planning to rob protection money collected from their affiliate stores...But it's a gang facility'; new says 'she and some accomplices will attempt to rob the protection money collected from those affiliated establishments' -- old mentions gang facility explicitly, new adds 'accomplices'.

TextENGNew: "The plan will fail.@k@vS14/05/210500417. Rina won't escape in time and will be 'secured' by the organization's men. As a warning to the others involved, she'll be killed."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: What recklessness. A chill ran down my spine as Rika-chan laid out the details in her matter-of-fact tone.
...Even I understood how insanely dangerous it was to steal from a yakuza organization's funds.
And the kind of retribution that awaited a failed attempt... I didn't even want to imagine.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Still, did that mean this Rina woman was in such dire financial straits that she felt she had no other choice...?
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Oh, but...@k@vS14/01/210100578. If Teppei comes back to Hinamizawa because of that, how does it connect to Satoko?"

"...If Teppei returns, he'll naturally try to move back into the Houjou house.@k@vS14/05/210500420. And since he can't do any housework himself, he'll seek out Satoko and drag her back as his live-in servant."
"...Actually, this particular chain of events has been quite rare across all the worlds I've experienced.@k@vS14/05/210500423. So the few times it happened, I simply wrote it off as bad luck."
"However," Rika-chan continued.
"Yesterday... I spotted Rina Mamiya at the library.@k@vS14/05/210500425. And I overheard something troubling."
It had been a coincidence.
Satoko had invited me to the library to find books for summer reading reports. And while we were there, I spotted 'her' -- Rina -- in the reading area.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to rina, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: Even from a slight distance, the pungent, garish scent of her perfume drifted over.
Her flashy, revealing outfit made it immediately clear what line of work she was in.
I'd only seen her face once, in a 'world' long past... but I would never forget it. Because it was this Rina Mamiya who had dragged both Satoko and Rena into misery.
Rina was with several young men. They were huddled around a circular table, heads close together, whispering intently.
The table was piled high with thick books, but it was obviously camouflage.
...The scene was clearly abnormal.
It was nearing evening, and the library was nearly empty. No one else was in the reading area.
Normally, Rina and her crowd would be making a disruptive racket wherever they went -- but would people like them even choose to gather in such a dim, unremarkable place?
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "Rikaaa, did you find 'Run, Melos!' yet?"
"Mii, not yet.@k@vS14/05/210500427. ...I'll look over here."
I hid behind a bookshelf, pretending to search for a book, and crept closer to where Rina and her group were sitting, straining my ears.
The Rina of this world shouldn't know my face, so I briefly considered a bolder approach -- walking into the reading area to grab a children's book. But I opted for caution.
Through the faint whispers, I could catch occasional stifled laughs. And when I'd gotten as close as I dared... I heard it clearly.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...But isn't this risky?@k@vS14/00/goroA10002. If we get caught skimming the protection money, it's definitely 'this' for us."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: The man tapped his own neck with his palm as he spoke. He was trying to play it off as a joke... but his slightly pale face made it less than convincing.
"In fact, I've heard the rumors.@k@vS14/00/goroB10002. ...And they say almost nobody's ever pulled it off."
"Then you're free to back out.@k@vS14/20/212100002. But your debts have piled up so high that your regular paycheck can't even cover the interest anymore. So what are you gonna do?"
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Y-yeah, but still..."
"Same goes for you.@k@vS14/20/212100004. And you.@k@vS14/20/212100005. You know what happens when you can't pay up, right?"
changeReason: New translation omits substantial content present in the original.

TextENGNew: "Sh-shut up.@k@vS14/00/goroC10002. Aren't you in the same boat?"
"Me?@k@vS14/20/212100009. Sorry, but I've got insurance.@k@vS14/20/212100010. Whether this job succeeds or fails, I've got a backup plan."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...Oh yeah?@k@vS14/00/goroA10004. You think that middle-aged deadbeat's really worth anything?"
"Middle-aged?@k@vS14/20/212100012. ...Oh, you mean Tetsu?@k@vS14/20/212100013. Ahaha, please. That useless lump couldn't even protect himself, let alone me."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Kyahahaha~!"
"...Hey, keep your voice down."
One of the men said that while nervously scanning the area. ...Our eyes almost met for an instant and my heart lurched, but he didn't bother with a child.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "Relax -- it's nothing but brats left in here.@k@vS14/20/212100017. Even if they heard us, they wouldn't understand a thing."
"Y-yeah, but..."

"What's with the nerves?@k@vS14/20/212100019. This is the one place in the area that has zero Sonozaki ties.@k@vS14/20/212100020. Or would you rather hold our meetings at the Okinomiya bars? Where every wall has ears?"
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: ...Stupid as always, this Rina.
True, the library was one of the few places outside the Sonozaki network... but did she not realize how conspicuously out of place her group looked?
If even one observant person connected to the Sonozakis noticed them, word would reach the higher-ups within days.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: The only ones oblivious to the danger were Rina and these foolish young men who had no real understanding of the Sonozaki family's terror.
...So this is how Rina Mamiya gets herself killed.
I wanted to tell this pathetic woman, strutting around like she owned the place, that she and her little crew were nothing more than insects dancing blissfully on a griddle about to be set ablaze.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Anyway... Pon, Yasuke.@k@vS14/20/212100022. You two, secure our getaway.@k@vS14/20/212100023. Shu-chan, you're in charge of scouting the route. Report back at the usual spot the day after tomorrow, seven PM sharp."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "R-right...!@k@vS14/00/goroC10004. Leave it to us."
"Hehehe... don't go getting cold feet.@k@vS14/20/212100026. Pull this off and we'll be rolling in money.@k@vS14/20/212100027. Then we can blow town, start fresh, live the good life... Right?"
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: Whether they had abandoned all pretense of secrecy or were simply too giddy to contain themselves, their furtive snickering was now perfectly audible. I turned on my heel and walked away with feigned indifference.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...Disgusting scum."
I gave my honest opinion. Rika-chan seemed to share the sentiment, pursing her lips in a bitter half-smile with a murmured '...Mii.'
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "But... even knowing all that, we can't just let her be killed.@k@vS14/01/210100582. The whole point is to stop Teppei from coming back and taking Satoko, right?"
I racked my brain, trying to think of some way to get ahead of this -- to make Rina and her group give up the robbery.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: Or, unpleasant as it was, maybe help them escape so they survived... No, that wouldn't work. Even alive, they'd flee the area. And then Teppei would still come back.
changeReason: New translation adds reference to teppei, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: Then...
"...No. It's better to let things proceed as they are."
"Huh?"
"If we stay silent, Rina Mamiya will go through with the robbery.@k@vS14/05/210500431. And then... she needs to die."
changeReason: Old says 'we'll let her die'; new says 'she needs to die'--different framing: passive allowing vs active necessity.

TextENGNew: Cold. Blunt.
Rika-chan took a somewhat reluctant gulp of cider and pronounced those words as though they were nothing.
changeReason: Translation differs significantly in the amount of detail conveyed.

TextENGNew: "...She needs to die."
changeReason: Old says 'We'll let her die'; new says 'She needs to die'--passive allowing vs active necessity.

TextENGNew: Words of shocking cruelty and ruthlessness, utterly unbecoming of Rika-chan. The ease and expressionlessness with which she said them was what stunned me most.
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: And besides --
"...B-but, Rika-chan?"
"Mii?"
"If Rina is killed, Teppei comes back to Hinamizawa, right?@k@vS14/01/210100586. And then Satoko gets dragged away by her uncle -- which is exactly what you want to prevent!"
"...I did say that.@k@vS14/05/210500435. Satoko will be taken away by Teppei.@k@vS14/05/210500436. And she'll suffer terribly."
changeReason: Old says 'Probably, almost certainly...'; new says 'And she'll suffer terribly.' Different concluding information.

TextENGNew: ``If that's the case, wouldn't it be better for us to do something to ensure Teppei won't return to Hinamizawa just because of Rina's disappearance?!@k@vS14/01/210100588. Or is there some secret plan we can use to keep Satoko from being taken away?``
changeReason: Substantially different translation that conveys the scene differently.

TextENGNew: "...There's no special plan for that.@k@vS14/05/210500438. Besides, Teppei is legally Satoko's guardian. If he demands her back, there's nothing she can do."
"?! You can't be serious! Then --!"
"-- Keiichi, please listen carefully."
Firmly.
changeReason: Old says 'Total rejection'; new says 'Firmly'--very different characterizations of tone (hostile vs authoritative).

TextENGNew: Rika-chan cut me off with a sharp tone and turned to face me with an earnest expression.
"Think about what I asked you.@k@vS14/05/210500441. I said to find the hardest path -- the one that leads to Satoko's greatest happiness."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "But!@k@vS14/01/210100591. Then why would you deliberately place Satoko with her uncle?!@k@vS14/01/210100592. That's the exact opposite of keeping her safe!"
changeReason: Translation uses substantially different phrasing.

TextENGNew: "...This is necessary. For Satoko's sake."
I couldn't make sense of it. How could forcing Satoko to live with the uncle who had tormented her possibly be 'for her sake'?
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "Maybe...@k@vS14/01/210100595. If they lived together again, would her uncle start treating her kindly?"
changeReason: New translation removes reference to satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...The complete opposite.@k@vS14/05/210500445. Every day, she'll be screamed at and worked to the bone. It'll be as bad as last year -- or worse."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "Are you mocking me, Rika-chan?!@k@vS14/01/210100597. And you still want me to hand Satoko over to that man?!"
"...Yes.@k@vS14/05/210500448. This is necessary -- for both Satoko and for you, Keiichi."
changeReason: New translation adds reference to keiichi, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: And then, Rika-chan explained.
After Satoko is taken by her uncle... the club members and I would unite with a single purpose -- to rescue her at any cost -- and storm the child consultation center.
But the consultation center wouldn't act, and day by day, Satoko would deteriorate further...
Unable to stand it any longer, I -- Keiichi Maebara -- would rally the entire village to speak out against Teppei's crimes and advocate for Satoko.
In the end, with the cooperation of the police and even the Sonozaki family -- who had long been hostile to the Houjou name -- Teppei would be arrested, and Satoko would be rescued.
"...What... is all that?"
"After Satoko is taken, it's the miracle that you and the others actually achieved."
"And through that struggle, you earned the trust of the entire village. And Satoko was finally, truly accepted by the people of Hinamizawa.@k@vS14/05/210500453. That is the best possible future."
It was all hypothetical. ...But Rika-chan spoke with such vivid certainty, as if she had witnessed every moment of it firsthand.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: And her face never broke into a smile -- not once. She was deadly serious throughout.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: She tried to keep her expression neutral, but the small fists clenched tight on her knees were slick with sweat and flushed red.
"Then... Rika-chan, what should I do?"
"...Even when something terrible happens to Satoko, please resist the urge to act rashly.@k@vS14/05/210500453. And if anyone around you seems about to lose control, hold them back."
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...And in the end, Satoko will be saved."
"So right now...@k@vS14/01/210100602. you're asking me to abandon Satoko?"
For the first time, a flame of anger blazed in Rika-chan's dark eyes. Seeing that reaction, I arrived at one certainty... and answered.
"...And after all that, Satoko will truly be saved?"
Rika-chan nodded, her expression still heavy. ...Even so, she couldn't quite shake the guilt from her eyes, and she sat staring down at the bottle in her hands.
"...Understood.@k@vS14/01/210100605. If that's truly the best path, I'll follow your lead.@k@vS14/01/210100606. But promise me one thing -- when it's all over, Satoko will be smiling."
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika and adds reference to satoko, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: "...Please.@k@vS14/05/210500458. Satoko will have to endure some pain for a while, but... I'm sure... in the end..."
Even as she spoke those words,
Rika-chan was gripping the bottle tightly, biting down hard on her lip.
The sight of her enduring that weight left me with nothing more to say. I drained my grape juice in a single, long gulp.
changeReason: New translation removes reference to rika, changing character attribution.

TextENGNew: ...The rawness in my throat probably wasn't just from the lukewarm sweetness.
We sat in silence for a long while. ...Eventually, noticing the daylight fading, I stood up.
changeReason: Translation uses different key terms that alter understanding.

TextENGNew: "...Well, I should head home.@k@vS14/01/210100608. See you tomorrow."
I left the shrine with a tangle of conflicting feelings.
Looking back as I descended the stone steps... Rika-chan was still sitting there, head bowed.
